reviews.review_title: If you like connect, you'll love it
reviews.review_description: I mean there can't be a better AR classic connect than this.

reviews.review_title: Great game, some suggestions for devs
reviews.review_description:
As a puzzle lover, this is great game, it's simple, to the point, calming. But I have some suggestions to make this even better.

UI: 
texts could use a little higher resolution. These kinds of games should always convey a feeling of delicacy, and resolution for certain elements is the key here. Carmack suggested devs should put majority of texts on a timewarp layer, maybe you guys can give it a try?
The depths of the holograms in the center of the level choosing menu is obviously incorrect, and it would cause a little bit eye strain, it's an easy fix. It could also benefit from a higher resolution or anti aliasing, since it should feel like an art piece, right now the shimmering is very distracting.
Overall, it's an intuitive UI and minimum which I like a lot.

Environment and music:
You guys are obviously going for an oriental zen vibe, which is very fitting, but I cannot remember any particular scene or track in the game, they are all kindda the same. Maybe you can introduce some chill or ambient types of environment and music, or change up some color combinations. also maybe check out Tetris Effect for some inspirations.

Puzzles: 
Sometimes if the puzzle have multiple solutions which is obviously the case here, I would feel less accomplished. For this kind of puzzles I think if you guys can develop a more refined way to let gamers to deduct the solution, not just through brutal trial and error, people would feel more engaged. Then some additional tools would be nice, like marks for certain color or cutters to darken the white line (sometimes I know certain "bridge" is not walkable). If all of these are done then you could have some kind of puzzle editor to let players upload their own puzzle, to prolong the life of this game.

I recommend this game to everyone I know that has a quest. I’ve completed every level over the course of a few evenings.

It’s a lovely spatial puzzle that really gets you thinking and using your spatial intuition and reasoning a bit like sudoku or Tetris. It’s calm and you can go at your own pace.

If you like puzzle games you will love Color Connect!

Color Connect is a puzzle game that has a simple concept and is easy to play.  The idea is that there are pairs of different colored spheres that you have to connect along the various paths while visiting each node along the way just once.  While it is easy to play, the puzzles can become complex as more color pairs, paths and nodes are introduced in later levels.  The game is quite forgiving as you can back up your moves as much as you want when you find the paths you have started don’t provide the solution.  The game grades you from 1 to 3 stars, with 3 stars awarded if you can trace the solution without having to back out any moves.  If you get less then 3 stars, you can replay a level trying to remember the final solution you just worked out, but with more complex puzzles it may be hard to remember all the correct paths.

While this game reminds me of a number of mobile games, it is beautifully realized in 3 dimensions and takes full advantage of virtual reality.  You can easily rotate and move the puzzles to best visualize what paths you want to draw.

The game is extremely polished. The environment and the relaxing background music are really lovely and properly set the tone to focus on and enjoy the puzzles.  The menus and the interface are beautifully designed and I appreciate how it allows you to incrementally make moves towards solving the puzzles (e.g., the way that the lines showing paths you can no longer take are removed).  

The puzzles are really fun and interesting.  For the first 20 or so puzzles I was able to logically work out every step I needed to take along the way without any guesses.  As the puzzles got increasingly more complex, I found I had to go through some trial and error to get to the solutions.  I am currently into the third puzzle set (10 puzzles each) and find this more challenging and still very fun.  There are plenty of puzzles yet for me to play and I expect things will get quite difficult.

This is a superb puzzle game that belongs on the main Oculus Store.  It reminds me of Cubism in a number of ways - a beautifully designed and challenging puzzle game with a lovely minimalistic interface.

Disclaimer: I was given a free code for a pre-release version of Color Connect.

developer_response.body: Wow! What a brilliant review. Very thorough and details the game well. We set out to create a relaxing yet engaging puzzle game unique to VR. Comments and reviews like this bring smiles to all of our faces. We're so glad youre still enjoying the game and look forward to adding more content to the game soon with future updates.

reviews.review_description:
Every once in a while you need a break from Shooters and Horror games 
Well this beautifully done puzzle game is the break you need , Different difficulty settings give you a ton of challenges  

Full 3D view of the puzzle 
Subtle music 
Stunning backdrop 
Seated or standing 
pick this up today

I have loved Mr time with this. Music is calming and has a similar vibe to the little cities soundscape. Graphics are beautifully stylised and don't distract from what you're doing, gameplay is really lovely. Definitely a few head scratchers in there too as well. Highly recommended
Great, simple concept yet increasingly complex puzzler that has you coming back for more.

This beautiful experience will relax and calm you with its surroundings and music and take your mind off of everything as you focus to complete each puzzle. I believe this game is good for stress management and may even be one of those games good for your brain. Well done and thank you Devs

This game truly found the balance between challenge and accessibility. As the levels progressed, I really enjoyed how the developers ramped up the difficulty - never too hard, never too easy. I played through all the main game puzzles, and am looking forward to any DLC. Give this game a go. It's easily one of the best puzzle games on the Quest. As a side note, there was nothing more satisfying than opening a puzzle and seeing that a color only had one possible direction - that always felt good.

Is it as replayable In Death: Unchained or as active as Thrill of The Fight? No, and it doesn't need to be. Color Connect fills its own important VR niche with calming yet engaging puzzles, immersive backgrounds and atmospheric music. This is a great game to play for 5 or 50 minutes at a time. 

A true hidden gem that's better than the majority of the titles in the main store. Grab it now.

l i n k t r . e e
/
q u a c k b i r t h

Video review here: https://youtu.be/7avbyGJ6xUE

This game is very aesthetically pleasing, which is an important first step that many titles neglect. By creating a peaceful space to solve the puzzles (and nice music), it increases the immersion and helps you get lost in the challenge. I'm not very good at puzzle games (which is evident in the video review), but I found the experience very relaxing and immersive. Great job!

developer_response.body:
We are really glad you enjoyed it! 
p.s. We actually thought you did quite well, practice makes perfect :)

reviews.review_title: A Relaxing Puzzle Game for the Quest
reviews.review_description:
Very relaxing puzzle game about drawing lines on constellations that you can enjoy from any position to get extra comfy. Check out the video for more in depth info but the key aspect is there is a beautiful view looking into space with some fog/cloud movement so it looks animated and nice and you can move the puzzle around anywhere so you can sit and relax anywhere you want without issue. 

Great experience to relax and I can definitely recommend it for those looking for that type of experience.

Video - https://youtu.be/1qXcwEPMWA0

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the positive review, we thoroughly enjoyed the video too!

reviews.review_description:
Simple to learn and hard to master. We had a blast reviewing Color Connect on our podcast Ruff Talk VR. With relaxing gameplay, soothing aesthetics, and a vast array of levels, this game is fun for the whole family!

Check out our full podcast review at www.RuffTalkVR.com

This is one of those hidden Gems that deserves to be on the official store.  I love that the devs created a new experience for puzzle games.  One that is relaxing, yet makes you think.  Good for all ages to play.  Not a battery drainer at all.  I totally recommend this game.

This is a very good game. I played about 15 levels, and the puzzles get really challenging every next level. Nice ambient music and bright colorful graphics. I also like the simplistic art-style; it really fits the game.

Three things I would like to see change:
1. Zooming in/out, rotating and moving with the grip buttons would nice. Maybe in a future update?
2. I was playing seated, and the starting position of the objects were too close in front of me. I had to move them every time. It would have been nice, that once I positioned the location, it is held kept in all further levels.
3. Another font for the title (Color Connect)? The current one is really boring. Or maybe you can add a small animation with coloured lines and connecting-dots around it?

Five stars. This game is definitely worth the money. Thumbs up!

(sorry for my english, its not my speaking language)

developer_response.body: We are really happy that you're enjoying Color Connect. Thank you so much for the positive review and also the suggested changes. All great ideas/points that we will be taking on board for future updates. Thanks again and happy gaming.

reviews.review_description: Super relaxing puzzle game, I'm really enjoying it.

reviews.review_title: Flow Free fans, don't miss this
reviews.review_description:
I sank so much time in Flow Free on my iPod touch and it's great to see a similar concept in 3D in VR. 

This is a very nice experience, puzzles are good (can't wait to unlock the more challenging ones) environments are relaxing and overall it has the right mix of challenge and chill.

Definitely deserves a spot in the main store! 

As a suggestion it would be cool to have a pass-through mode as it is very nice for this kind of games (like in cubism)
The game starts easy and gets more difficult with every level. It makes you think. I really like that you can move and resize the puzzles.

Color Connect is a very enjoyable and beautiful puzzle game. Simple, but wonderful backgrounds and calm ambient music help the player to have a moment of relief and fully focus on connecting colorful dots through spatial labirynths. I dare to say that this can even put in some kind of a meditative state. Game is really immersive and sharpens player's perception skills. Puzzles require a sense of space, but once you find a solution, the game rewards you by the very satisfying view of shapes made from neon lines. I really recommend this hidden gem.
